segments.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,
If we assay to commune with thee, wilt thou be grieved? but who can withhold himself from speaking?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Behold, thou hast instructed many, and thou hast strengthened the weak hands.
Thy words have upholden him that was falling, and thou hast strengthened the feeble knees.
But now it is come upon thee, and thou faintest; it toucheth thee, and thou art troubled.
Is not this thy fear, thy confidence, thy hope, and the uprightness of thy ways?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Remember, I pray thee, who ever perished, being innocent? or where were the righteous cut off?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Even as I have seen, they that plow iniquity, and sow wickedness, reap the same.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: By the blast of God they perish, and by the breath of his nostrils are they consumed.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

footnotes: by the breath…: that is, by his anger

segments.text: The roaring of the lion, and the voice of the fierce lion, and the teeth of the young lions, are broken.
The old lion perisheth for lack of prey, and the stout lion's whelps are scattered abroad.
Now a thing was secretly brought to me, and mine ear received a little thereof.
In thoughts from the visions of the night, when deep sleep falleth on men,
Fear came upon me, and trembling, which made all my bones to shake.
Then a spirit passed before my face; the hair of my flesh stood up:
It stood still, but I could not discern the form thereof: an image was before mine eyes, there was silence, and I heard a voice, saying,
Shall mortal man be more just than God? shall a man be more pure than his maker?
segments.speaker: Unknown Spirit

footnotes: there…: or, I heard a still voice

segments.text: Behold, he put no trust in his servants; and his angels he charged with folly:
segments.speaker: Unknown Spirit

footnotes: nor in his angels, in whom he put light

segments.text: How much less in them that dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, which are crushed before the moth?
segments.speaker: Unknown Spirit

segments.text: They are destroyed from morning to evening: they perish for ever without any regarding it.
segments.speaker: Unknown Spirit

segments.text: Doth not their excellency which is in them go away? they die, even without wisdom.
segments.speaker: Unknown Spirit
